title: Boston Marathon finisher got her tooth chipped by her medal
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Marathon “It was just such a crazy incident,” she says.
Alexis Taladay bites her medal after chipping her tooth on it.
Courtesy of Alexis Taladay Unlike most runners, whose marathon experiences culminate in a medal and a celebration after finishing 26.

title: 4 things to know about Patriots 7th-round pick Jam Miller
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: New England Patriots At 5-foot-10, 221 pounds, Miller runs with power and has a low center of gravity.
Jam Miller's first name is actually Jamarion, but he prefers to go by "Jam" for simplicity purposes.
AP Photo/Vasha Hunt The Patriots selected Alabama running back Jam Miller in the 7th round of this year’s draft.

title: Sex offender accused of hiding in women’s bathroom at Northeastern, assaulting officers
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime Prosecutors say Brandon Awogboro thrashed his legs and attempted to bite officers who arrested him Wednesday.
Pedestrians walk near a Northeastern University sign on the school's campus in Boston.

Rodrique Ngowi for the AP, File A Boston sex offender with a lengthy criminal record was back in court Thursday after prosecutors say he hid in a women’s bathroom stall on Northeastern University’s campus and spied on an employee who came in to use the restroom.

title: South Boston’s Marian Manor redevelopment to bring 204 apartments with 160+ parking spaces
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Local News Plans call for a “full gut renovation” of the former nursing home, including the construction of a new 164-spot parking garage.
The former Marian Manor nursing home in South Boston closed in 2024, and will be the site of a new 204-unit apartment complex with 160+ parking spaces.

David L Ryan/Globe Staff The former Marian Manor nursing home in South Boston is set to be transformed into a 204-unit rental apartment complex, offering a potential boost to a neighborhood and a city grappling with a deepening housing crisis.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s status for Game 7
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Boston Celtics Tatum is expected to play, Mazzulla said.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla (right) tries to figure out what's going wrong with forward Jayson Tatum during the second quarter.

Danielle Parhizkaran/Globe Staff Jayson Tatum is expected to play in the Celtics’ matchup against the 76ers in Game 7 of the Eastern Conference first round at TD Garden on Saturday, according to coach Joe Mazzulla.

title: What is Driveline Baseball, and how is it shaping the Red Sox organization?
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Boston Red Sox "It’s hard to describe everything we’re doing unless you see it.
" Red Sox Chief Baseball Officer believes in Kyle Boddy's methods and Driveline's purpose.
Barry Chin/Globe Staff ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: 2 teens arrested following massive fire that destroyed mill in Haverhill
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime A 17- and 18-year-old are suspected of setting a fire that caused so much damage to the mill that it is slated to be demolished, police say.
Two teens allegedly set a fire to a mill at 14 Stevens St.
Haverhill Fire Department Two teenagers were arrested following a multi-alarm fire at an abandoned Haverhill mill building Tuesday, authorities said.

title: Tractor-trailer driver killed in Danvers crash identified as Mass. father of 9
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News Justin Obert was “the very heartbeat and pulse of this company,” Tasha Trucking & Propane said.
Tractor-trailer driver Justin Obert was killed in a rollover crash in Danvers Monday.
GoFundMe The tractor-trailer driver killed Monday in a rollover crash on the North Shore has been identified as 61-year-old Justin Obert, a Plymouth father of nine.

title: Paul McCartney was just in Rhode Island to screen his new doc for Brown students
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Celebs The former Beatle’s new album drops in just a few weeks.
Paul McCartney performs at the Glastonbury Festival in Worthy Farm, Somerset, England, on June 25, 2022.

title: Medical examiner changes Sandra Birchmore’s manner of death from ‘suicide’ to ‘undetermined’
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Former Stoughton police detective Matthew Farwell allegedly killed Birchmore in 2021 and staged her death to appear as if it were a suicide.
Sandra Birchmore in a picture posted to social media on January 9, 2021.
